answer:
<think> To answer this question, I need to recall the timeline of William Whitelaw's career in the Conservative Party. I know that William Whitelaw was a prominent figure in the party, serving as Home Secretary and Deputy Leader under Margaret Thatcher. I also recall that Edward Heath was the leader of the Conservative Party from 1965 to 1975. The second shadow cabinet of Edward Heath was formed in 1965, when Heath became leader. I need to determine whether Whitelaw was already a deputy leader at that time or not. I know that Whitelaw became Deputy Leader in 1975, after Heath resigned as leader. Therefore, he was not the deputy leader before the formation of the second shadow cabinet of Edward Heath.</think>
<answer> after </answer>
